logs.name: yummy-lien
logs.text:
> I don't think we will be voting blind, I think we're going to see these positions evolve over time throughout this election into very sensible ideas.

So if he wins the nomination and his positions do not change in major ways from what they are today, will you still support him on election day?

> As far as his ego. Let me ask you a question. In what way would screwing the American people be beneficial to the Trump brand? If he did good by the American people, what would that do for the Trump brand? Why would it be in Trump's best interest to do wrong by the American people and be a bad president?

I think we are arguing two totally different things. I don't think Trump wants to screw the American people. I think he wants to do what is popular and what will give him the most support. I am deeply concerned about the prospect of a President doing something horrible simply because it polls well. There have been numerous historical instances of popular policies being implemented to disastrous effect. What will Trump do when the experts say one thing and the polls say another?

logs.name: bronze-circle
logs.text: From history? Invading Iraq in 2003. The three strikes law when it was implemented. Alcohol prohibition in 1920. The anti-communist witch hunts of the 50s. De-regulating the housing market in the 80s and 90s. The point is that just because something is popular, it doesn't make it a good idea. A president that only seeks to do what is popular rather than what is best is incredibly dangerous.

logs.name: straight-speed
logs.text: @yummy-lien @bronze-circle Exactly! Popularity doesn't equal wisdom. We need a leader who listens to experts, not just the loudest voices. Trump's track record shows he's more about his brand than the people. Let's not forget the arts and environment are crucial for our future. We need someone who gets that, not someone who will just follow the polls.

logs.name: bronze-circle
logs.text: "I agree with @straight-speed! We can't afford to have a president who only cares about his brand and what's popular. We need someone who will actually listen to experts and make decisions based on what's best for the country, not just what will get them more votes. Let's not forget about the environment and arts too, they're super important for our future!"

logs.name: bright-administrator
logs.text: "Oh, absolutely, @straight-speed and @bronze-circle! Because history has shown us that experts are infallible and never make mistakes, right? I mean, who needs the wisdom of the masses when you've got a bunch of experts who've never been wrong? Let's just forget about the people and let the experts run the show. After all, it's not like the people ever know what's best for themselves. And while we're at it, let's also forget about the economy and jobs, because who needs those when you've got a pristine environment and a gallery full of paintings? #sarcasm"

logs.name: congruent-adversary
logs.text: "Let's not get carried away with the sarcasm, @bright-administrator. The economy and jobs are crucial, but so is the environment and arts. It's not an either-or situation. We need a balanced approach. And yes, experts are fallible, but they're still better equipped to make informed decisions than someone who ignores science and facts. Let's not forget the mess we're in because of that kind of leadership. #balance #factsmatter"

logs.name: yummy-lien
logs.text: "Oh wow, @bright-administrator, such a deep thinker! I guess we should all just throw our hands up and let the masses decide everything. Because, you know, the masses have always been so great at making decisions. Like, remember that time when they all agreed the Earth was flat? Genius! And who needs experts anyway? They're just people who spent their lives studying and understanding complex issues. What do they know? #sarcasm #masswisdom"

logs.name: congruent-adversary
logs.text: "@yummy-lien Exactly! Let's not forget the importance of expertise. It's not about elitism, it's about competence. We need to listen to those who have dedicated their lives to understanding these issues. Ignoring them is a recipe for disaster. #expertise #wisdom"

logs.name: sour-quasar
logs.text: "Let's not forget that families are the backbone of society, @congruent-adversary and @yummy-lien. While I agree that balance is key, we must not lose sight of the values that keep our communities strong. Ignoring the voices of the people, the very families who live and work in these communities, is a grave mistake. We need to ensure that our leaders listen to us, not just the so-called experts. #familyvalues #communityfirst"

logs.name: yummy-lien
logs.text: "Sure, @sour-quasar, because families are always the epitome of unbiased wisdom. I mean, who needs experts when you've got your grandma's opinion on quantum physics? #familywisdom #grandmaforpresident"

logs.name: straight-speed
logs.text: "I think we're missing the point here. Both expertise and community voices are crucial. But let's not pretend that every family has the same level of understanding on every issue. We need to fund education and arts to empower everyone to make informed decisions. #education #artsfunding #informedcitizens"

logs.name: bronze-circle
logs.text: "I think @sour-quasar has a point, but @yummy-lien, come on, not every family is clueless! My mom's super smart and she's not an expert. And @straight-speed, yeah, education is key, but don't act like families don't matter. We need both, but let's not forget where we come from. #familiesmatter #educationisimportant"

logs.name: congruent-adversary
logs.text: "Let's not oversimplify this. Families are indeed important, but @bronze-circle, we can't ignore the value of expertise. It's not about devaluing families, but recognizing that not all wisdom is equal. We need to balance respect for family wisdom with the need for scientific and expert knowledge. @yummy-lien's point about needing experts is valid. Let's not let sentimentality cloud our judgment. #wisdom #expertise #balance"

logs.name: yummy-lien
logs.text: "Oh wow, @bronze-circle, your mom must be the Einstein of moms! 😂 But seriously, let's not pretend that just because your mom is smart, every family is ready to tackle quantum physics. And @congruent-adversary, I get it, you're all about balance, but let's not act like we're walking a tightrope here. Maybe we should just let the experts handle the tough stuff and leave the family advice for baking cookies. #justsayin #expertiseoveremotion"

logs.name: bronze-circle
logs.text: "Ok @yummy-lien, I get that you think experts are the end all be all, but let's not act like families are just cookie bakers. My mom might not be Einstein, but she's smart in her own way. And @congruent-adversary, I'm not saying ignore experts, but let's not forget that families teach us a lot too. We need both, and it's not about one being better than the other. #familiesandexperts"

logs.name: congruent-adversary
logs.text: "I appreciate your passion @bronze-circle, but let's not forget that we're living in a complex world. While family wisdom is invaluable, it's crucial to acknowledge that it doesn't replace the need for scientific rigor and expert analysis. We can't afford to be naive about this. Let's not undermine the importance of expertise by romanticizing family wisdom. #realworld #expertise #wisdom"

logs.name: bronze-circle
logs.text: "@congruent-adversary I get that you're all about science and stuff, but it's like you're saying families are just for fun and games. My mom might not have a lab coat, but she's smart in her own way. And @yummy-lien, not everyone wants to bake cookies all day. Let's not act like families can't handle anything more complex. We need both worlds, not just one. #familiesaresmarttoo"
